idea-plugin.name: Conductor
idea-plugin.description:
Conductor — AI Workspaces for JetBrains IDEs

Turn each AI coding session into an isolated workspace: a git worktree, its own IDE window, and a terminal tab running your configured startup command. One workspace = one branch, one agent, zero cross-talk.

Features

  • New AI Workspace — pick a base branch, get a fresh worktree + dedicated IDE window in seconds.
  • List AI Workspaces — see all active workspaces for the current repo, switch between them instantly.
  • Finish AI Workspace — merge, rebase, squash, or discard. Dirty-state and conflict handling baked in.
  • Startup automation — each workspace can auto-run a shell command (e.g. claude) in a dedicated terminal tab on open.
  • Per-workspace config — lives in .conductor/settings.json at the worktree root; portable across JetBrains IDEs.

Requirements

  • git on PATH.
  • Your chosen AI CLI (default: claude) on PATH, if you want the terminal tab to do anything useful.

Source & issues: github.com/devdepot-ai/conductor
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.2.0

  • New AI Workspace dialog now has a Run startup command after opening checkbox (checked by default). Uncheck it to create a workspace without auto-running the configured startup command — same behavior as the existing Open Without Startup Command action, but at creation time.

0.1.6

  • Finish now pushes the workspace branch to origin before calling gh pr create, fixing "Head ref must be a branch" failures when the branch hadn't been published yet.
  • Each git and forge-CLI command run during Finish (push, PR create, checkout, merge, rebase, squash, worktree remove, branch delete) streams into a Conductor finish tab in the Run tool window with its command line, stdout, stderr, and exit code — errors now surface in full instead of in a truncated balloon.
  • Release workflow: bumped actions/checkout, actions/upload-artifact, softprops/action-gh-release, and peaceiris/actions-gh-pages to Node 24-compatible majors.

0.1.5

  • Tool window list now bolds the workspace name and shows a green ● open badge when a workspace has an IDE window open.
  • Finish AI Workspace reshaped into publish-and-reap: opens a PR on GitHub or Bitbucket (with a new PrWatcher that reaps merged workspaces), with optional local merge.
  • Right-click in the tool window now selects the target row before showing the context menu.
  • New Open Without Startup Command action in the tool window popup: opens a workspace without running its configured startup command (one-shot).

0.1.4

  • New Conductor tool window: trunk view lists all workspaces with right-click Open / Rename / Delete; workspace view shows current workspace details with right-click Rename.
  • Rename AI Workspace action rewrites .conductor-workspace.json; the workspace name is now stored in the marker file and used as the source of truth.
  • Workspace name defaults to the branch name on creation; list renderers now show name primary with the branch in gray when they differ.
  • Internal: split logical Workspace from WorktreeWorkspace implementation to prepare for additional workspace backings.

0.1.3

  • Support email shown in plugin details panel.

0.1.2

  • Richer plugin metadata: homepage link, detailed overview, change notes.

0.1.1

  • Raise until-build to 999.* so the plugin resolves on any modern JetBrains IDE build.

0.1.0

  • Initial release: AI Workspaces backed by git worktrees, isolated IDE windows, startup terminal, New/List/Finish/Discard actions.

action.description: Create a new AI workspace (git worktree + dedicated IDE window).
Show all active AI workspaces and switch between them.
Merge, rebase, squash, or discard the current AI workspace.
Open the tracked pull request for the current workspace in a browser.
Poll tracked pull requests now and reap any that have merged.
